title: The Family Table
narration: There used to be six chairs at this table. Every Sunday, all of them were full. Grandpa at the head. Mom next to the kitchen so she could keep checking the rice. My cousin and I kicking each other under the table. Now there's four chairs and most Sundays they're empty. Nobody moved away. We're all still here. We just stopped showing up. I don't know when it happened. One Sunday became two. Then it was just holidays. Then not even that. The table's still there though. Still has the scratch from when I dropped the blender.
